SAN ANTONIO WELLNESS & REHABILITATION

CCN: 455762 · SAN ANTONIO, TX 78247 · Bexar County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
ONE HEARTLAND DR, SAN ANTONIO, TX 78247
Phone
2106531219
Certified beds
154
Avg daily residents
125 (81% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1988-07-11
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.32 4.09 3.14 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.35 0.72 0.33 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.16 0.91 1.10 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.82 2.47 1.71 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.51 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.84
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.32
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.69
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
28.8%
annual
RN turnover
58.3%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.45
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-08 12 5 7 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-10-06 35 18 35 301 1 301
Total weighted health score
117.3
lower is better
Infection-control citations
4
last cycle

Deficiencies (51)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

50 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2026-01-16 2026-01-17
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2025-09-11 2025-09-12
0603 Protect each resident from separation (from other residents, his/her room, or confinement to his/her room). D 2025-09-11 2025-09-12
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-09-11 2025-09-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-07-02 2025-07-03
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-07-02 2025-07-03
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-07-02 2025-07-03
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-11-08 2024-11-09
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-11-08 2024-11-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-11-08 2024-11-09
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2024-11-08 2024-11-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-08 2024-11-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-10-18 2024-10-19
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. E 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-01-16 2024-01-17
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-12-03 2024-01-10
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. K 2023-12-03 2023-12-04
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-12-03 2023-12-04
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-12-03 2024-01-10
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. E 2023-10-06 2023-11-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-09-21 2023-10-13
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2023-08-23 2023-08-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-08-23 2023-08-24
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-04-07 2023-04-08
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-04-07 2023-04-08
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-03-01 2023-03-02
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-03-01 2023-03-02
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-08-26 2022-08-27
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2022-08-26 2022-08-27

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.

  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
